specs.section_path: > 5.2 Embodied SCPN: Cellular, Neural, & Systemic Implementation > III. The Neuro-Visceral Axis: The Architecture of Embodiment (L5) > 2. The Gut-Brain Axis (GBA) and the Microbiome: The Deep Milieu
source_formulae: P0R04911: 2. The Gut-Brain Axis (GBA) and the Microbiome: The Deep Milieu
P0R04912: The GBA integrates the CNS with the Enteric Nervous System (ENS) and the Gut Microbiome.

P0R04914: Fig.: GBA modulation map. The microbiome shapes brain-body dynamics via three pathways: L2 tuning (DA/5-HT/GABA precursors), L3/L4 modulation (BDNF; E/I balance -> criticality), and immune regulation (systemic inflammation tone). Vagal signalling links ENS CNS (Insula/ACC) bidirectionally.
P0R04915: [IMAGE:Ein Bild, das Text, Diagramm, Reihe, Schrift enthlt. KI-generierte Inhalte knnen fehlerhaft sein.]

P0R04916: Fig.: Gut-Brain Axis (GBA) & Microbiome. The microbiome acts as an external regulator with three levers: L2 tuning (precursors for DA, 5-HT, GABA), L3/L4 modulation (BDNF; E/I balance -> criticality), and immune regulation (systemic inflammation tone). Vagal signalling provides a bidirectional conduit between ENS and CNS (Insula/ACC). Pathology - Dysbiosis: inflammation -> BBB breakdown -> L1-L4 disruption (Dyscritia & Decoherence).

P0R04917: The Microbiome as an External Regulator: The microbiome profoundly modulates brain function: L2 Tuning: Produces precursors for DA, 5-HT, and GABA. | L3/L4 Modulation: Influences plasticity (BDNF) and E/I balance (Criticality). | Immune Regulation: Modulates systemic inflammation. | Vagal Signalling: The Vagus nerve is the primary conduit for bidirectional signalling. | Pathology (Dysbiosis): Alterations in the microbiome (Dysbiosis) are linked to psychiatric and neurodegenerative disorders. Dysbiosis induces inflammation, leading to BBB breakdown and disruption of L1-L4 dynamics (Dyscritia and Decoherence).
P0R04919: Fig.: Dysbiosis cascade. Dysbiosis -> inflammation -> BBB breakdown -> L1-L4 disruption (Dyscritia, Decoherence) -> elevated psychiatric/neurodegenerative risk.
